name: academic-stress-test-pipeline
description: Fail-closed HEP thesis stress-test workflow: local quote extraction, verification gate, external academic search, and adversarial synthesis package.

inputs.target_thesis_path: Path to the uploaded thesis or paper PDF/text file.
inputs.chapters: Default: 4,5. Use explicit pages when chapter headings cannot be found.

hard_constraints: Do not run external search until exact local quotes pass the verification gate.
Stop immediately if the local parse drifts to generic non-HEP topics such as Neural Networks, PINNs, or Fluid Dynamics.
Do not update canonical science files from this workflow unless the user accepts specific evidence-ledger changes.

steps.name: literal-local-parse
steps.prompt: Run deployment/deer-flow/skills/custom/academic-stress-test/scripts/academic_stress_test_pipeline.py against {{target_thesis_path}} with --chapters {{chapters}} or --pages {{pages}}. This step may read only the target local file and must produce extraction.json.

steps.name: verification-gate
steps.prompt: Inspect {{extraction}}. Continue only if gate.passed is true. If false, report the exact missing anchors and stop without external search.

steps.name: external-academic-audit
steps.prompt: If {{gate_status}} passed, use the verified extracted claim queries to run targeted searches for LHC, ATLAS, CMS, ALICE, PYTHIA baseline limitations, OHP/MIX reference-sample bias, and exponential/Levy/tau fit-quality comparisons.

steps.name: adversarial-synthesis-package
steps.prompt: Use the generated synthesis_input.md plus the original target file to produce a defense audit report with a claim alignment table, systematic gaps, baseline-comparison gaps, and five defense questions.
